title: AvaTrade Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of AvaTrade pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
AvaTrade Pricing Overview

AvaTrade offers a refreshingly transparent pricing model: all account types are completely free to open and maintain. Whether you're a beginner exploring the markets or a professional trader, there are no monthly subscription fees, no platform charges, and no account maintenance costs. The firm makes its money through spreads on trades rather than direct fees, which aligns with standard forex broker pricing across the industry.

Founded in 2006 and headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, AvaTrade is regulated by multiple major financial authorities worldwide. The company serves over 300,000 traders across forex, indices, commodities, stocks, crypto, ETFs, bonds, and options markets. With a minimum deposit of just $100 and access to industry-standard platforms like MT4, MT5, and proprietary tools, AvaTrade positions itself as an accessible entry point for beginners while offering depth for experienced traders.

All AvaTrade Plans Compared

AvaTrade operates a straightforward, three-tier account structure with zero monetary differences:

Account Type Monthly Cost Minimum Deposit Key Features
Demo Account $0/month N/A (Virtual) Practice with virtual funds; all platforms available; no time limit; ideal for learning
Standard Account $0/month $100 Live trading on all markets; spreads from ~1.8 pips on major pairs; access to MT4, MT5, AvaOptions; copy trading enabled
Professional Account $0/month $100 Same features as Standard; available to experienced traders; typically lower spreads (broker discretion); enhanced risk management tools

The absence of tiered subscription fees is a major advantage. Unlike some competitors that charge $49–$299/month for professional accounts or premium features, AvaTrade doesn't restrict platform access or trading tools behind paywalls. All three account types provide access to:

  • MT4 and MT5 platforms
  • Copy trading via DupliTrade and AvaSocial
  • Automated trading and algorithmic tools
  • Mobile and desktop trading
  • Real-time alerts and news feeds
  • SharpTrader education platform

Free Plan / Free Trial

AvaTrade's Demo Account is a true freemium offering. You receive:

  • $10,000 (or more) in virtual trading capital
  • Access to all platforms (MT4, MT5, AvaTradeGO)
  • Real-time market data and price feeds
  • Unlimited practice trading—no expiration date
  • Zero pressure to deposit funds

This is genuinely useful for learning. Unlike many brokers that time-limit demo accounts to 30 days, AvaTrade allows indefinite practice. The demo mirrors live market conditions, spreads, and execution, so what you learn transfers directly to real trading. It's an excellent sandbox for backtesting strategies, testing copy trading features, or familiarizing yourself with MetaTrader before committing capital.

Is it worth starting with? Yes, absolutely. New traders should spend weeks or months on the demo before depositing. However, be aware that demo trading is risk-free and emotionless—live trading with real money introduces psychological pressures that paper trading doesn't capture.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While AvaTrade advertises zero account fees, several costs apply once you begin trading:

Spreads (the primary cost)

  • Major forex pairs: ~1.8 pips average
  • Minor pairs and exotics: 2–5+ pips
  • Crypto pairs: Wider spreads, especially off-peak hours
  • Stocks and indices: Variable, typically 1–3 points depending on instrument

These spreads are wider than ECN/STP-focused brokers like Interactive Brokers or Axiory (which offer 0.1–0.5 pip spreads plus small commissions). For day traders executing 10–20 trades daily, AvaTrade's spreads can be costly over time.

Inactivity fees

This is a significant hidden cost: if your account remains inactive for 3 months, AvaTrade charges inactivity fees of $10–$50/month (amount varies by region and account state). If you deposit $1,000 and don't trade for a year, you could lose 10–50% of your capital to inactivity alone. This aggressively incentivizes trading or account closure.

Withdrawal fees

  • Wire transfers: May incur bank fees (typically $10–$30)
  • Credit/debit card: Usually free
  • E-wallets (Skrill, Neteller): Free withdrawals

Overnight swap fees

Holding positions overnight incurs overnight swap fees (interest charges). These vary by instrument and direction of the trade. For forex, swaps typically range from −5 to +10 pips per day, depending on the currency pair and whether you're long or short.

Premium data feeds and tools

Advanced charting tools, premium news feeds, and proprietary indicators may carry small costs, though most are included with the base account.

AvaTrade Pricing vs Competitors

AvaTrade's pricing model is competitive but not the cheapest in the forex space. Here's how it compares to direct alternatives:

Broker Account Fee Spreads (EURUSD) Minimum Deposit Best For
AvaTrade $0/month ~1.8 pips $100 Beginners, copy traders, multi-asset traders
Interactive Brokers $0/month* 0.1–0.3 pips + commission $0–$500 (varies) Active traders, professionals, low-cost priority
eToro $0/month* 0.8–2.0 pips $10–$200 Social/copy trading, CFD traders, beginners
XM Global $0/month 1.4–2.0 pips $5–$50 Micro traders, bonus hunters, beginners

*Interactive Brokers charges $10/month unless you trade enough; eToro is free but includes wider spreads or withdrawal fees for some regions.

AvaTrade's positioning:

  • vs. eToro: Identical spread range; AvaTrade edges out for MetaTrader purists and those avoiding proprietary platforms
  • vs. Interactive Brokers: IB is cheaper for high-volume traders (0.1 pips + $3 commission per 100k), but requires more capital and is complex for beginners
  • vs. XM Global: XM is slightly cheaper on spreads (1.4 vs 1.8 pips) and lower minimum deposit ($5), but offers fewer educational resources and copy trading features

Is AvaTrade Worth the Price?

Honest assessment: AvaTrade offers solid value for specific trader profiles—not the absolute cheapest, but well-rounded.

Best for AvaTrade:

  • Beginner forex traders: Free demo account, low $100 minimum, excellent SharpTrader education platform, and no confusing fee structures make it ideal for learning
  • Copy traders: DupliTrade and AvaSocial integration; built-in copy trading removes friction
  • Multi-asset traders: Forex, stocks, indices, crypto, and ETFs all in one account with identical pricing—no need to jump between brokers
  • Mobile-first traders: AvaTradeGO mobile platform is intuitive and feature-rich
  • Options traders: AvaOptions platform is dedicated and well-integrated

When to avoid AvaTrade:

  • High-frequency traders: 1.8 pip spreads are expensive at scale; use Interactive Brokers instead
  • Scalpers: AvaTrade doesn't allow scalping or use of arbitrage bots; they'll restrict or close your account
  • Long-term buy-and-hold investors: Use a stock broker like Fidelity; CFD spreads aren't designed for long-term holds
  • Dormant account holders: The 3-month inactivity fee ($10–$50/month) will erode small accounts quickly

Value verdict: At $0/month, AvaTrade's entry cost is unbeatable. You pay only via spreads, which are competitive but not tier-leading. The real value lies in educational resources, copy trading, and multi-asset access. For beginners and intermediate traders prioritizing ease of use and learning, the value justifies the slightly wider spreads versus ultra-low-cost competitors.

How to Save on AvaTrade

1. Use the demo account aggressively

Spend months practicing before depositing. Avoid early mistakes that cost real money. The demo is genuinely unlimited, so there's no rush to go live.

2. Start with micro-lots ($1,000 deposits, 0.01 lot trades)

You can begin with the $100 minimum, but spreads take proportionally larger bites from small accounts. Build capital on the demo first, then deposit enough to trade with at least 0.1 lot sizing to reduce spread impact.

3. Avoid inactivity fees

Trade at least once every 3 months, or close the account if you're stepping away. If you anticipate dormancy, use the demo account instead.

4. Optimize trade timing

Spreads widen during low-liquidity hours (3 AM–6 AM EST, Asian session starts). Trade during high-volume hours (8 AM–5 PM EST) when spreads are tightest.

5. Use limit orders instead of market orders

Market orders execute at the spread; limit orders can fill inside the spread during busy trading hours, potentially saving 0.2–0.5 pips per trade.

6. Avoid overnight holds when possible

Swap fees compound. Day-trade when feasible to avoid overnight costs. If you must hold overnight, review swap rates by pair beforehand and choose pairs with favorable overnight rates.

7. Join AvaTrade's affiliate or partner programs

AvaTrade offers rebate programs for high-volume traders. If you consistently trade 5+ lots monthly, inquire about commission-sharing arrangements—not advertised publicly but available for qualifying accounts.

8. Leverage copy trading for passive income

Instead of active trading, use AvaSocial or DupliTrade to copy experienced traders automatically. This reduces your trading costs (you pay spreads only on copy trades, not your own erratic trades) and potentially improves returns through strategy diversification.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
